name: NVIDIA Isaac Lab / Isaac Sim (execution & demo layer)
role: Runs the EXECUTION / DEMO only: N rigid-body cars, each driven by the frozen high-level coordination policy -> frozen low-level controller -> car. NOT used for the multi-agent training (that's the kinematic env).
decision: Use Isaac for the physics hero shot because WheeledLab is Isaac-native. Instantiate many cars in one scene; each car runs the two frozen policies (no learning at demo time, so single-robot-per-env limits don't bite the training). The coordination policy was trained cheaply on the kinematic env; Isaac only replays it with real physics.
outputs: rigid-body car rollouts (RTX-rendered frames + trajectories) for the demo
hard_constraints: GPU MUST have RT Cores -> A100/H100/H200/B200 NOT supported. Only RTX-class / L40S / RTX-PRO-6000 work. You need such a box for the demo render.
Ubuntu 22.04 or Windows 11 only (NO macOS); ~50GB+ disk; 16GB+ VRAM.
Headless cloud render needs Vulkan/EGL to init -> if rendering on Modal, test vulkaninfo in an L40S container first.
scope_note: Confined to execution/demo keeps Isaac's heavy footprint off the critical training loop. If the demo box falls through, you can still ship the kinematic-env render (see viz-demo) and lose only the physics flair.
risks: Isaac install/setup time -> start it early, it is the long pole for the demo
imported USD road geometry dropping colliders in some 2025 builds -> verify contacts
alternatives: kinematic-env render as a fallback demo if no RTX box is available
effort: hours-to-a-day of setup; only for the demo, not the training
